symptoms.action: Check soil drainage first. Apply nitrogenous fertilizer if growth is slow. Use ferrous sulfate foliar spray for interveinal yellowing.

crop_specific.wheat: Yellow stripe rust is Puccinia striiformis. Spray tebuconazole or propiconazole immediately. Acts fast in cool and moist weather.

general_causes: Fungal rust disease spread by wind
symptoms.action: Apply systemic fungicide such as tebuconazole at first sign. Repeat after 14 days if disease continues.

crop_specific.cotton: Wilting in cotton may indicate Fusarium wilt or severe water stress. Inspect roots for discoloration.
crop_specific.tomato: Bacterial wilt causes sudden wilting even with adequate moisture. No cure; remove infected plants.

symptoms.action: Irrigate if soil is dry. If wet soil and wilting, suspect root rot or vascular wilt. Uproot and inspect roots.

crop_specific.cotton: Check inside squares and bolls for pink bollworm or American bollworm larvae. Dropping bolls indicate active infestation.
crop_specific.maize: Fall armyworm hides inside the whorl. Check whorl for frass and larvae.

general_causes: Pink bollworm or American bollworm larval infestation
Water stress during boll development stage
Excessive heat causing physiological boll shedding
Nitrogen overdose promoting vegetative growth over fruiting
symptoms.action: Open dropped bolls and inspect for larvae. Apply chlorpyrifos or spinosad if larvae present. Verify irrigation schedule at boll development stage.

general_causes: Humid conditions with warm days and cool nights
symptoms.action: Spray sulfur-based fungicide or wettable sulfur. Improve plant spacing. Avoid excess nitrogen which creates dense growth.

crop_specific.wheat: Loose smut in wheat produces black powder replacing grain. Use fungicide-treated seed next season.
crop_specific.maize: Common smut produces galls that turn black and dusty.

symptoms.action: Remove and destroy infected plants. Use certified treated seed next season. Apply seed treatment fungicide before sowing.

crop_specific.tomato: Fruit cracking caused by irregular watering. Consistent irrigation prevents cracking.
crop_specific.onion: Bulb splitting caused by sudden water after dry period.

general_causes: Irregular or sudden heavy irrigation after dry spell
symptoms.action: Maintain consistent irrigation schedule. Apply boron as micronutrient. Avoid alternating dry and wet periods.

crop_specific.cotton: Cotton leaf curl virus is spread by whitefly. Control whitefly with imidacloprid or thiamethoxam.

symptoms.action: Control vector insects immediately. Remove and destroy infected plants. Use resistant varieties in future. No chemical cure for viruses.

crop_specific.rice: Brown leaf tips in rice may indicate potassium deficiency or leaf scorch disease.

symptoms.action: Apply muriate of potash if potassium deficient. Check soil salinity. Irrigate if drought stress. Avoid overdose of nitrogen.

crop_specific.wheat: Russian wheat aphid causes leaf rolling and discoloration. Spray imidacloprid or dimethoate.
crop_specific.cotton: Aphids weaken plants and secrete honeydew attracting sooty mold.

general_causes: Spread rapidly in warm dry weather
symptoms.action: Spray imidacloprid, dimethoate, or insecticidal soap. Repeat after 7 days. Avoid excess nitrogen which promotes soft growth preferred by aphids.
